conventions: Peer-multiple relative valuation — fair value is the average of the per-multiple implied prices. Valuation only — no live market price is used.

input.fundamentals_source: The FY2027 figures below are Wall Street analyst consensus estimates — the model does no forecasting of its own. Consensus is unusually split this cycle (FY2027 EPS estimates run from ~$98 to ~$150), so the forward figure is the softest, highest-impact input: change the estimate you feed in and the fair value moves directly.

drivers.label: EPS $115
drivers.comment: Middle of a split FY2027 consensus (~$98 to ~$150). This forward figure is the main swing factor.

drivers.label: P/E 7×
drivers.comment: About a 35% premium to peers' ~5× — MU's long-run average premium for its US listing and better shareholder returns. We deliberately stop there rather than layering an AI re-rating on top, to stay consistent with the peer-level EV/EBITDA (a business re-rating would have to lift that too).

drivers.label: EV/EBITDA 8×
drivers.comment: Roughly in line with peers (~8×) and MU's own long-run median.

drivers.label: P/B 4×
drivers.comment: Book value is a weak yardstick here — MU's very high ROE means a peer-like P/B understates it, so this row reads as a floor.
